query: Trace the evolution of the concept 'body without organs' from Anti-Oedipus (1972) through A Thousand Plateaus (1980), showing how its meaning shifts from a psychoanalytic critique to a practical ethics. Cite specific passages from each work.
works_required: 1972 Anti-Oedipus Capitalism and Schizophr - Deleuze, Gilles.pdf
expected_stages: AO: anti-production, repulsion of organs, schizophrenic body
ATP: experimentation, caution, types of BwO, practical how-to
label: BwO evolution AO→ATP

query: Show how Deleuze's treatment of 'repetition' develops from Difference and Repetition through to the cinema books. How does the concept change when applied to film? Provide cited passages demonstrating the shift.
expected_stages: DR: bare vs clothed repetition, difference in itself
C1: movement-image repetition
C2: time-image, crystal-image, direct time
label: Repetition DR→Cinema

query: Trace how the concept of 'deterritorialization' shifts between Anti-Oedipus and A Thousand Plateaus. In AO it relates to capitalism's decoded flows; in ATP it becomes a general principle of transformation. Cite specific passages showing this drift.
works_required: 1972 Anti-Oedipus Capitalism and Schizophr - Deleuze, Gilles.pdf
expected_stages: AO: capitalism decodes and deterritorializes flows
ATP: theorems of deterritorialization, relative vs absolute, reterritorialization
label: Deterritorialization AO→ATP

query: Document the evolution of Deleuze's concept of 'the virtual' from Bergsonism through Difference and Repetition to Cinema 2. Show with citations how the virtual moves from Bergsonian duration to ontological principle to cinematic time.
expected_stages: Bergsonism: virtual as coexistent past, cone of memory
DR: virtual as differential, Idea, intensive
C2: crystal-image, virtual-actual circuit
label: Virtual Berg→DR→C2

query: Show how Deleuze's engagement with Nietzsche's 'will to power' in Nietzsche and Philosophy (1962) transforms into the concept of 'desire' in Anti-Oedipus (1972). Cite passages from both works demonstrating the conceptual lineage.
works_required: 1972 Anti-Oedipus Capitalism and Schizophr - Deleuze, Gilles.pdf
expected_stages: NP: will to power as affirmation, active/reactive forces
AO: desiring-production, desire as production not lack
label: Will to power → Desire NP→AO

query: Trace the concept of 'the diagram' across Francis Bacon (1981), Foucault (1988), and A Thousand Plateaus (1980). Show how the same term operates differently in painting, power relations, and semiotics. Cite each usage.
works_required: 1981 Francis Bacon The Logic of Sensation - Deleuze, Gilles.pdf
expected_stages: FB: diagram as catastrophe in painting, dismantling cliche
Foucault: diagram as abstract machine of power
ATP: diagrammatic component of pragmatics
label: Diagram FB→Foucault→ATP

query: Document how the concept of 'sense' (sens) develops from The Logic of Sense to What Is Philosophy?. In the former it operates at the surface between propositions and things; in the latter it becomes the event of the concept. Cite passages from both.
works_required: 1969 The Logic of Sense - Deleuze, Gilles.pdf
expected_stages: LS: sense as surface effect, incorporeal, event
WIP: concept as event, plane of immanence
label: Sense LS→WIP

query: Show the evolution of Deleuze's concept of 'immanence' from Spinoza: Practical Philosophy through What Is Philosophy?. Trace how the plane of immanence shifts from an ethical ontology to a philosophical method. Cite both works.
expected_stages: Spinoza: immanence as Ethics vs morality, joyful affects
WIP: plane of immanence as pre-philosophical ground of thought
label: Immanence Spinoza→WIP

query: Trace how 'the fold' operates across The Fold (1988) and the Foucault book (1988). In one it describes Leibnizian metaphysics; in the other it describes subjectivation. Show with citations how the same figure does different philosophical work.
works_required: 1988 The Fold Leibniz and the Baroque - Deleuze, Gilles.pdf
expected_stages: Fold: infinite fold of matter and soul, monad, Baroque
Foucault: fold as subjectivation, inside of the outside
label: Fold: Leibniz vs Foucault

query: Document how Deleuze's concept of 'signs' changes from Proust and Signs (1964) to A Thousand Plateaus (1980). In the earlier work signs are objects of an apprenticeship; in the later they become components of regimes. Cite passages showing the transformation.
expected_stages: PS: four types of signs, apprenticeship, involuntary memory
ATP: regimes of signs, signifying/post-signifying, pragmatics
label: Signs PS→ATP
